title: quercus phellos
intro: Quercus phellos, also known as the willow leaf oak or swamp white oak, is a deciduous tree species native to North America. The bark, leaves, and roots of Quercus phellos have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Quercus phellos include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Quercus phellos can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
